extra.message: Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into the return value of ping, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).

Unsanitized input from the HTTP request body flows into the return value of unserialize, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into the return value of get_user, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Unsanitized input from the HTTP request body flows into pickle.loads, where it is used to deserialize an object. This may result in an Unsafe Deserialization vulnerability.

Unsanitized input from data from a remote resource flows into the return value of requests_example, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Do not hardcode passwords in code. Found hardcoded password used in PASSWORD.
Running the application in debug mode (debug flag is set to True in run) is a security risk if the application is accessible by untrusted parties.
